heading: Directors of national research institutes
label: 284 Directors of national research institutes

Federal Research, , , provided that:Pub. L. 117–215, title IV, § 401136 Stat. 2265Dec. 2, 2022“(a).—In GeneralNot later than 1 year after the date of enactment of this Act [], the Secretary of Health and Human Services, in coordination with the Director of the National Institutes of Health and the heads of other relevant Federal agencies, shall submit to the Caucus on International Narcotics Control, the Committee on the Judiciary, and the Committee on Health, Education, Labor, and Pensions of the Senate and the Committee on Energy and Commerce and the Committee on the Judiciary of the House of Representatives a report on—Dec. 2, 2022“(1)the potential therapeutic effects of cannabidiol or marijuana on serious medical conditions, including intractable epilepsy;“(2)the potential effects of marijuana, including—“(A)the effect of increasing delta-9-tetrahydrocannabinol levels on the human body and developing adolescent brains; and“(B)the effect of various delta-9-tetrahydrocannabinol levels on cognitive abilities, such as those that are required to operate motor vehicles or other heavy equipment; and“(3)the barriers associated with researching marijuana or cannabidiol in States that have legalized the use of such substances, which shall include—“(A)recommendations as to how such barriers might be overcome, including whether public-private partnerships or Federal-State research partnerships may or should be implemented to provide researchers with access to additional strains of marijuana and cannabidiol; and“(B)recommendations as to what safeguards must be in place to verify—“(i)the levels of tetrahydrocannabinol, cannabidiol, or other cannabinoids contained in products obtained from such States is accurate; and“(ii)that such products do not contain harmful or toxic components.“(b).—ActivitiesTo the extent practicable, the Secretary of Health and Human Services, either directly or through awarding grants, contacts, or cooperative agreements, shall expand and coordinate the activities of the National Institutes of Health and other relevant Federal agencies to better determine the effects of cannabidiol and marijuana, as outlined in the report submitted under paragraphs (1) and (2) of subsection (a).”[For definitions of terms as used in , set out above, see , set out as a note under , Food and Drugs.]section 401 of Pub. L. 117–215section 2(a) of Pub. L. 117–215section 801 of Title 21

Enhancing the Clinical and Translational Science Award, , , provided that:Pub. L. 109–482, title I, § 106120 Stat. 3696Jan. 15, 2007“(a).—In GeneralIn administering the Clinical and Translational Science Award, the Director of NIH shall establish a mechanism to preserve independent funding and infrastructure for pediatric clinical research centers by—“(1)allowing the appointment of a secondary principal investigator under a single Clinical and Translational Science Award, such that a pediatric principal investigator may be appointed with direct authority over a separate budget and infrastructure for pediatric clinical research; or“(2)otherwise securing institutional independence of pediatric clinical research centers with respect to finances, infrastructure, resources, and research agenda.“(b).—ReportAs part of the biennial report under section 403 of the Public Health Service Act [], the Director of NIH shall provide an evaluation and comparison of outcomes and effectiveness of training programs under subsection (a).42 U.S.C. 283“(c).—DefinitionFor purposes of this section, the term ‘Director of NIH’ has the meaning given such term in section 401 of the Public Health Service Act [].”42 U.S.C. 281

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Director of the National Cancer Institute shall be appointed by the President, and the Directors of the other national research institutes and national centers shall be appointed by the Secretary, acting through the Director of National Institutes of Health. Each Director of a national research institute or national center shall report directly to the Director of National Institutes of Health.

children.heading: Term
children.label: A Term
children.text: A Director of a national research institute or national center who is appointed by the Secretary, acting through the Director of National Institutes of Health, shall be appointed for 5 years.

children.heading: Reappointment
children.label: B Reappointment
children.text: At the end of the term of a Director of a national research institute or national center, the Director may be reappointed in accordance with standards applicable to the relevant appointment mechanism. There shall be no limit on the number of terms that a Director may serve.

children.heading: Vacancies
children.label: C Vacancies
children.text: If the office of a Director of a national research institute or national center becomes vacant before the end of such Director’s term, the Director appointed to fill the vacancy shall be appointed for a 5-year term starting on the date of such appointment.

children.heading: Current directors
children.label: D Current directors
children.text: Each Director of a national research institute or national center who is serving on , shall be deemed to be appointed for a 5-year term under this subsection beginning on such date.December 13, 2016

children.heading: Rule of construction
children.label: E Rule of construction
children.text: Nothing in this subsection shall be construed to limit the authority of the Secretary or the Director of National Institutes of Health to terminate the appointment of a director referred to in subparagraph (A) before the expiration of such director’s 5-year term.

children.heading: Nature of appointment
children.label: F Nature of appointment
children.text: Appointments and reappointments under this subsection shall be made on the basis of ability and experience as it relates to the mission of the National Institutes of Health and its components, including compliance with any legal requirement that the Secretary or Director of National Institutes of Health determines relevant.

children.heading: Nonapplication of certain provision
children.label: 3 Nonapplication of certain provision
children.text: The restrictions contained in section 202 of the Departments of Labor, Health and Human Services, and Education, and Related Agencies Appropriations Act, 1993 (; note) related to consultants and individual scientists appointed for limited periods of time shall not apply to Directors appointed under this subsection.Public Law 102–39442 U.S.C. 238f

children.heading: Duties and authority; grants, contracts, and cooperative agreements
children.label: b Duties and authority; grants, contracts, and cooperative agreements
i
children.text: the maintenance of health,

children.label: ii
children.text: the detection, diagnosis, treatment, and prevention of human diseases and disorders,

children.label: iii
children.text: the rehabilitation of individuals with human diseases, disorders, and disabilities, and

children.label: iv
children.text: the expansion of knowledge of the processes underlying human diseases, disorders, and disabilities, the processes underlying the normal and pathological functioning of the body and its organ systems, and the processes underlying the interactions between the human organism and the environment;

children.label: B
children.text: may, subject to the peer review prescribed under and any advisory council review under , conduct the research, investigations, experiments, demonstrations, and studies referred to in subparagraph (A);section 289a(b) of this titlesection 284a(a)(3)(A)(i) of this title

children.label: C
children.text: shall, as appropriate, conduct and support research that has the potential to transform the scientific field, has inherently higher risk, and that seeks to address major current challenges;

children.label: D
children.text: may conduct and support research training (i) for which fellowship support is not provided under , and (ii) which is not residency training of physicians or other health professionals;section 288 of this title

children.label: E
children.text: may develop, implement, and support demonstrations and programs for the application of the results of the activities of the institute to clinical practice and disease prevention activities;

children.label: F
children.text: may develop, conduct, and support public and professional education and information programs;

children.label: G
children.text: may secure, develop and maintain, distribute, and support the development and maintenance of resources needed for research;

children.label: H
children.text: may make available the facilities of the institute to appropriate entities and individuals engaged in research activities and cooperate with and assist Federal and State agencies charged with protecting the public health;

children.label: I
children.text: may accept unconditional gifts made to the institute for its activities, and, in the case of gifts of a value in excess of $50,000, establish suitable memorials to the donor;

children.label: J
children.text: may secure for the institute consultation services and advice of persons from the United States or abroad;

children.label: K
children.text: may use, with their consent, the services, equipment, personnel, information, and facilities of other Federal, State, or local public agencies, with or without reimbursement therefor;

children.label: L
children.text: may accept voluntary and uncompensated services; and

children.label: M
children.text: may perform such other functions as the Secretary determines are needed to carry out effectively the purposes of the institute.

children.label: A
children.text: may enter into a contract for research, training, or demonstrations only if the contract has been recommended after technical and scientific peer review required by regulations under ;section 289a of this title

children.label: i
children.text: if the direct cost of the grant or cooperative agreement to be made does not exceed $50,000, such grant or cooperative agreement may be made only if such grant or cooperative agreement has been recommended after technical and scientific peer review required by regulations under , andsection 289a of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: if the direct cost of the grant or cooperative agreement to be made exceeds $50,000, such grant or cooperative agreement may be made only if such grant or cooperative agreement has been recommended after technical and scientific peer review required by regulations under and is recommended under by the advisory council for the national research institute involved; andsection 289a of this titlesection 284a(a)(3)(A)(ii) of this title

children.label: C
children.text: shall, subject to , receive from the President and the Office of Management and Budget directly all funds appropriated by the Congress for obligation and expenditure by the Institute.section 300cc–40c(d)(2) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: review and make the final decision with respect to making the award; and

children.label: i
children.text: the mission of the national research institute or national center and the scientific priorities identified in the strategic plan under ;section 282(m) of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: programs or projects funded by other agencies on similar research topics; and

children.label: iii
children.text: advice by staff and the advisory council or board of such national research institute or national center.

children.heading: Coordination with other public and private entities; cooperation with other national research institutes; appointment of additional peer review groups
children.label: c Coordination with other public and private entities; cooperation with other national research institutes; appointment of additional peer review groups

children.text: shall coordinate, as appropriate, the activities of the institute with similar programs of other public and private entities;
shall cooperate with the Directors of the other national research institutes in the development and support of multidisciplinary research and research that involves more than one institute;

children.label: A
children.text: establish technical and scientific peer review groups in addition to those appointed under ; andsection 282(b)(16) of this title

children.label: B
children.text: appoint the members of peer review groups established under subparagraph (A); and
may publish, or arrange for the publication of, information with respect to the purpose of the Institute without regard to .section 501 of title 44
